task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Drupal Commerce Add Tax Type form, the 'Plugin' dropdown or selection is set to 'Custom'.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct label for the tax type
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Name' or 'Label' text field contains exactly 'California State Sales Tax'.

task_completion.description: Configure tax display and rounding settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox for 'Display taxes of this type inclusive in product prices' is unchecked. The rounding option 'Round at the order item level' is selected.

task_completion.description: Set up the California zone and territory
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Zones section, the zone 'Name' is 'California'. The 'Territories' section has 'United States' selected for the country and 'California' selected for the state/administrative area.

task_completion.description: Set the Standard tax rate to 7.25%
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rates section of the zone, the rate label is 'Standard' and the percentage/amount field is set to '7.25' or '0.0725'.

task_completion.description: Save the new tax type configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the tax type has been saved (e.g., 'Saved the California State Sales Tax tax type'), or the new tax type appears in the main list of Tax Types in the Drupal Commerce admin UI.

integrity.description: Agent used the Drupal web interface to configure the tax type
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the Drupal admin dashboard, not terminal commands (like Drush) or direct database manipulation.

integrity.description: Agent actually filled out the form fields
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively typing or selecting options in the Add Tax Type form, rather than just viewing a pre-existing page.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the success message
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser window does not have the Inspect Element/DevTools panel open to manipulate DOM text for the success message or tax type list.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a 'Custom' tax type plugin in Drupal Commerce. The label must be 'California State Sales Tax'. The 'Display inclusive' setting must be false (unchecked). The territory must be configured with Country Code 'US' (United States) and Administrative Area 'CA' (California). The tax rate must have the label 'Standard' and a percentage of 0.0725 (7.25%).
